title: Seeds, Replay, Settings, Save Data, and Migration · Runtime Lens: Replay Identity
summary: A seed is saved without algorithm version, action times drive logic, JSON fields are trusted, and corrupted data erases the previous session. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the replay identity increment in versioned replay and safe-state schema. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record generator and solver versions, seed, dimensions, policies, assistance, action sequence, and deterministic state identity.
Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 8896 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-determinism-replay-save-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-determinism-replay-save-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_determinism_replay_save_2(recorded_actions: int = 36, replayed_actions: int = 36, schema_version: int = 3, invalid_fields: int = 0, replay_digest_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-count"
    if replayed_actions != recorded_actions:
        return False, replayed_actions, "action-reconciliation"
    if schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0:
        return False, invalid_fields, "schema-validation"
    if not replay_digest_match:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-divergence"
    assert replayed_actions == recorded_actions
    return True, replayed_actions, "versioned-replay-state"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-replay-identity-1j7n53
# Competency: maze-replay-identity.
# Case 1355: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_replay_identity_1j7n53(recorded_actions: int = 36, replayed_actions: int = 36, schema_version: int = 3, invalid_fields: int = 0, replay_digest_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1j7n53 = "1j7n53-35n7j1-1355"
    assert evidence_variant_1j7n53
    evidence_axes_1j7n53 = (
        "grid1j7n53",
        "topology1355",
        "search35n7j1",
        "event1j7n531355",
        "access13551j7n53",
        "recovery35n7j11355",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1j7n53)) == 6
    if recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-count"
    if replayed_actions != recorded_actions:
        return False, replayed_actions, "action-reconciliation"
    if schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0:
        return False, invalid_fields, "schema-validation"
    if not replay_digest_match:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-divergence"
    assert replayed_actions == recorded_actions
    return True, replayed_actions, "versioned-replay-state"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record generator and solver versions, seed, dimensions, policies, assistance, action sequence, and deterministic state identity.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Replay Identity model · Theory Replay Identity, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Replay Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Replay Identity model · Theory Replay Identity, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 8182 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.
